assessment: The dashboard clearly wants to be a single-screen daily pulse, and the KPI strip + three-column pulse row lays out the right primitives (orders, cash, inventory, appointments, AR/AP). But the execution buries the single most important signal on the page — 'You're short $551,981.62 after scheduled bills' — inside the middle of a side column, while top-line KPIs sit without any trend/comparison so a user cannot tell if 511 open orders or $963,104 AR is good or bad. The 'Today at a Glance' card is rendering as three gray skeleton bars with no resolution and no empty-state copy, which reads as broken. The left nav duplicates itself (SALES QUICK ACTIONS + SELL both surface ordering/catalogue entry points), and the page has no H1 at all — the most prominent heading is 'Command Palette' from the search dialog. Strong bones, weak hierarchy and unfinished states.
notable_strengths: Five-card KPI strip gives a scannable top-of-page pulse with consistent iconography and unit subtext ('38,185 live units', '41 open invoices').
Cash Position panel does the hard work of showing Cash on Hand → Scheduled Payables → Available After Bills as a connected story with a red callout, which is exactly the owner's decision framing.
Per-widget error/empty boundaries are respected — the Appointments card degrades to a proper illustrated empty state ('No upcoming appointments') instead of a blank tile.

findings.title: 'Today at a Glance' is stuck on skeleton bars with no resolution
findings.what_is_wrong: The 'Today at a Glance' card renders three gray placeholder bars below 'Sales, cash, and inbox signals for your morning review.' with no copy, no data, and no empty state. It looks identical to a permanent loading state.
findings.why_it_matters: The owner's first stop is literally labeled 'for your morning review' — if it renders as dead skeletons, the user assumes the dashboard is broken and loses trust in the other KPI numbers on the same page.
findings.proposed_fix: Give OwnerQuickCardsWidget a real empty-state copy block ('No signals needing attention — nice.') with a muted icon and a secondary 'Open Notifications' link, matching the Appointments card's treatment. If the widget is still fetching, replace bars with a single spinner + label 'Loading today's signals…' so loading is distinguishable from empty.
findings.functionality_preserved: OwnerQuickCardsWidget and its ComponentErrorBoundary stay intact — only the loading/empty render path is replaced; the drill-through 'Open Notifications' link and data contract are untouched.

findings.title: The cash shortfall — the single most important signal — is buried
findings.what_is_wrong: 'You're short $551,981.62 after scheduled bills.' sits inside the right-hand Cash Position card, visually equivalent to the Appointments empty state next to it. The KPI strip above it shows CASH COLLECTED (7D) $0 with no link to this shortfall.
findings.why_it_matters: The baseline says this page is the owner's 'daily pulse' for 'cash decisions'. A -$551K position is the decision of the day and should be the first thing the eye lands on, not the fourth column of the third row.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a full-width, dismissable alert row immediately under the header when Available After Bills < 0: red-tinted bar reading 'Cash short $551,981.62 after scheduled bills — review $551,981.62 in payables' with 'Review Bills' + 'Pay Bills' buttons. Keep the Cash Position card below exactly as-is for the full breakdown.
findings.functionality_preserved: OwnerCashDecisionPanel and its Review Bills / Manage links remain; the banner is a new surface that reads from the same computed value, so no tRPC or business logic changes.

findings.title: Top KPIs have no trend or comparison, so numbers are uninterpretable
findings.what_is_wrong: Four of the five KPI cards (OPEN ORDERS 511, FULFILLED TODAY 0, OUTSTANDING RECEIVABLES $963,104, INVENTORY VALUE $34.2M) show only a number and a supporting unit. Only CASH COLLECTED (7D) carries a comparison, and even that one renders the awkward '— N/A vs last week'.
findings.why_it_matters: An owner cannot tell from '511 open orders' whether the business is accelerating or stalling. Without a delta, the KPI row is decorative instead of diagnostic — it fails the 'what needs your attention today' promise in the subheader.
findings.proposed_fix: Add a single-line delta under every KPI subtext: '▲ 14 vs yesterday', '▼ $42K vs 7d avg', etc. For FULFILLED TODAY 0 show '0 of 511 open — behind typical 09:00 pace'. Replace '— N/A vs last week' with 'First week of tracking' when the comparator is null.
findings.functionality_preserved: OperationalKpisWidget (TER-1055) and its drill-through links to filtered workspaces are untouched; this is a subtext addition sourced from the same data the widget already renders.

findings.title: Left nav duplicates itself between 'SALES QUICK ACTIONS' and 'SELL'
findings.what_is_wrong: The sidebar shows SALES QUICK ACTIONS (New Order, Active Orders, Sales Catalogue, Recent Customers, Today's Shipments) stacked directly above a SELL group (Sales, Demand & Supply, Leaderboard). A user looking for 'create order' has two plausible homes.
findings.why_it_matters: Every other nav group (BUY, OPERATIONS, FINANCE) uses the collapsed-section pattern, so the flat QUICK ACTIONS block breaks the pattern and forces the user to scan twice. It also eats vertical space on smaller laptops, pushing SELL's children off-screen.
findings.proposed_fix: Keep QUICK ACTIONS but move it to a compact 2-column icon grid pinned at the top, above the section list, labeled 'Quick actions' (sentence case). Inside SELL, surface Sales, Demand & Supply, Leaderboard as-is. Do not delete any link — same five quick-action targets, just visually separated as shortcuts rather than a parallel taxonomy.
findings.functionality_preserved: All five quick-action routes (New Order, Active Orders, Sales Catalogue, Recent Customers, Today's Shipments) and all three SELL children remain navigable; only visual grouping changes.

findings.title: Page has no H1; screen readers and page-title scanners see 'Command Palette'
findings.what_is_wrong: Playwright reports H1: [] and the only H2 is 'Command Palette'. The visible 'OWNER COMMAND CENTER' is a styled badge, not a heading element.
findings.why_it_matters: Assistive tech and browser outline tools will announce the command-palette dialog as the page's main landmark. It's also an accessibility audit failure the team will have to fix eventually.
findings.proposed_fix:
Promote 'Owner Command Center' to an

rendered to the left of the badge styling (visually the badge can remain), and demote the date subhead to

. Add an aria-live region around the 'Live · Updated 12:08 PM' chip so refreshes are announced.
findings.functionality_preserved: The OWNER COMMAND CENTER badge component and the 'Live · Updated HH:MM' freshness indicator both keep their current behavior and styling; only the underlying semantic tag changes.

findings.title: 'Live · Updated 12:08 PM' freshness signal is too quiet and ambiguous
findings.what_is_wrong: The freshness chip sits at the far right edge of the header row in thin gray text. There is no indication of refresh cadence, whether it auto-refreshes, or how to force a refresh. Cash Position separately shows 'Updated 04/23 12:08 PM' in its own footer — two clocks, no hierarchy.
findings.why_it_matters: On a dashboard whose entire value prop is 'what needs your attention today', the user needs to trust that the numbers are current before they act on a -$551K shortfall.
findings.proposed_fix: Move the freshness chip immediately under the H1, styled as 'Live • updated 12:08 PM • refreshes every 60s' with a small ⟳ icon button that manually refreshes. Remove the duplicate 'Updated 04/23 12:08 PM' line inside Cash Position; rely on the page-level chip.
findings.functionality_preserved: The per-widget data refetch on mount (OwnerCashDecisionPanel etc.) is untouched; the manual refresh button invokes the same existing queries.

findings.title: Empty-state copy is inconsistent between KPI cards
findings.what_is_wrong: FULFILLED TODAY 0 gets warm copy 'Nothing shipped yet'. CASH COLLECTED (7D) $0 gets the technical 'Last week $0 — N/A vs last week'. The two tell the same story ('no data to compare yet') in different voices.
findings.why_it_matters: Inconsistent voice on KPIs that sit inches apart reads as half-finished product and erodes the polish of the whole strip.
findings.proposed_fix: Standardize: when the primary value is 0 and there is no comparator, render '<friendly copy> — <helpful next step or context>'. E.g., 'No cash collected this week yet', 'First 7 days of tracking'. Strip the em-dash '—' arrow glyph when the delta is unknowable.
findings.functionality_preserved: OperationalKpisWidget's value computation is untouched; only the empty/zero-state string template changes.

findings.title: Floating action button (bottom right) and 'Pay Bills' / 'Review Bills' / 'View Clients' actions compete without hierarchy
findings.what_is_wrong: There is an unlabeled orange circular FAB in the bottom-right corner, plus 'Review Bills →', 'Pay Bills', 'View Clients →', 'Manage →', and 'View all →' scattered across the same fold. The FAB has no tooltip visible and no clear relationship to the page.
findings.why_it_matters: On a decision dashboard, ambient CTAs should guide the eye toward the one thing that matters today. Five peer-level arrows plus an unlabeled FAB produce choice paralysis.
findings.proposed_fix: Give the FAB a visible tooltip and icon-meaning on hover (e.g., 'New order'). Demote secondary card links ('View all', 'Manage', 'View Clients') to tertiary styling (muted, no arrow glyph). Keep 'Review Bills' as the only primary-styled in-card CTA since it's tied to the cash shortfall narrative.
findings.functionality_preserved: Every drill-through link named in the baseline (per-widget drill-through and the FAB action) continues to route to the same destination; only weight/tooltip changes.

redesign_sketch:
Header row:

Owner Command Center

with OWNER COMMAND CENTER pill inline, subhead 'Thursday, Apr 23 — here's what needs your attention today', freshness meta on the next line ('Live • updated 12:08 PM • auto-refreshes every 60s ⟳'). Immediately below, a conditional full-width red alert banner when Available After Bills < 0: 'Cash short $551,981.62 after scheduled bills' with [Review Bills] [Pay Bills] buttons right-aligned. Then the existing five-card KPI strip, each card upgraded with a delta line ('▲ 14 vs yesterday' or 'First week of tracking' when null). Row 3 — three equal columns: Today at a Glance (with real empty-state copy and an icon, not skeleton bars) · Appointments (unchanged) · Cash Position (unchanged, but drop its duplicate 'Updated…' footer since freshness is now in the header). Row 4 — Money In vs. Out · Suppliers Waiting to Be Paid (unchanged). Row 5 — Inventory Snapshot · Aging Inventory. Row 6 — collapsed OwnerSkuStatusBrowserWidget. Left nav: pin a compact 'Quick actions' icon grid (New Order, Active Orders, Sales Catalogue, Recent Customers, Today's Shipments) above the section list, then SELL / BUY / OPERATIONS / RELATIONSHIPS / FINANCE / ADMIN as consistent collapsible groups. Microcopy rules: use sentence case for all card CTAs, reserve primary button styling for Review Bills only, standardize zero/empty states to friendly + actionable ('No cash collected this week yet' instead of '$0 — N/A vs last week').
